revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''志々伎神社'''（しじきじんじゃ）は[[長崎県]][[平戸市]]にある神社。志自伎神社とも表記する。旧[[県社]]。 ==概要== ...'
revision.text:
'''志々伎神社'''（しじきじんじゃ）は[[長崎県]][[平戸市]]にある神社。志自伎神社とも表記する。旧[[県社]]。
==概要== 創建年代は不明であるが。神域は[[平戸島]]の南端近くにある[[志々伎山]]（標高347.2m）を中心としており、古くは山の頂上に上宮、中腹に中宮、麓にある漁村の宮の浦に地の宮（下宮、邊都宮）、そして宮の浦沖の沖ノ島に沖の宮がそれぞれ置かれ、別に別当寺として円満寺が設けられていた。祭神は[[日本武尊]]の子、[[仲哀天皇]]の異母弟で、[[神功皇后]]の[[三韓征伐]]に従い、その後当地に留まり西国警護を命ぜられたと伝えられる[[十城別王]]（十城別命）で、地の宮は王が設けた武器庫を転用したもの、沖の宮は王の墓所であると伝えられている。
「[[延喜式]]」神名帳において[[肥前国]]内では4社のみの[[式内社]]の一つとしてその名があり、長崎県内でも最も古くから知られた神社である。その後も[[松浦党]]諸家や周辺地域漁民の崇敬を広く集めた。
現在の祭祀等の中心は中宮となっている。当初志々伎山山頂下の祠にあったが、[[1559年]]（永禄2年）に中腹に、さらに[[1961年]]（昭和36年）に明治初年に[[神仏分離令]]により廃された円満寺の跡地である現在地に移った。
「旧式内社志々伎神社跡」として長崎県史跡に指定されている。
==関連項目== *[[神社一覧]] *[[くんち]]
{{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ししきしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:式内社]] [[Category:平戸市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=志々伎神社| 画像=| 所在地=長崎県平戸市野子町251| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|10|44.34|N|129|22|8.62|E|}}| 祭神=十城別王| 社格=式内社（小）
旧県社| 創建=不詳| 本殿=| 例祭=| 神事= }} '''志々伎神社'''（しじきじんじゃ）は[[長崎県]][[平戸市]]にある神社。志志伎神社あるいは志自伎神社とも表記する。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。 ==概要== 創建年代は5世紀頃とも言われるが、正確には不明である。神域は[[平戸島]]の南端近くにある[[志々伎山]]（標高347.2m）を中心としており、古くは山の頂上に上宮、中腹に中宮、麓にある漁村の宮の浦に地の宮（下宮、邊都宮）、そして宮の浦沖の沖ノ島に沖の宮がそれぞれ置かれ、別に別当寺として円満寺が設けられていた。祭神は[[日本武尊]]の子、[[仲哀天皇]]の異母弟で、[[神功皇后]]の[[三韓征伐]]に従い、その後当地に留まり西国警護を命ぜられたと伝えられる[[十城別王]]（十城別命）で、地の宮は王が設けた武器庫を転用したもの、沖の宮は王の墓所であると伝えられている。 「[[延喜式]]」神名帳において[[肥前国]]内では4社ある[[式内社]]の一つとしてその名があり、壱岐対馬を除く長崎県内では唯一であり、最も古くから知られた神社である。その後も[[松浦党]]諸家や周辺地域漁民の崇敬を広く集めた。 現在の祭祀等の中心は中宮となっている。当初志々伎山山頂下の祠にあったが、[[1559年]]（永禄2年）に中腹に、さらに[[1961年]]（昭和36年）に明治初年に[[神仏分離令]]により廃された円満寺（当社の[[別当寺]]）の跡地である現在地に移った。 旧社殿地は[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に「旧式内社志々伎神社跡」として長崎県史跡に指定されている。 ==関連項目== *[[神社一覧]] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ししきしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:肥前国の式内社]] [[Category:平戸市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=志々伎神社| 画像=| 所在地=長崎県平戸市野子町251| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|10|44.34|N|129|22|8.62|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| 祭神=十城別王| 社格=式内社（小）
旧県社| 創建=不詳| 本殿=| 例祭=| 神事= }} '''志々伎神社'''（しじきじんじゃ）は[[長崎県]][[平戸市]]にある神社。志志伎神社あるいは志自伎神社とも表記する。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。 ==概要== 創建年代は5世紀頃とも言われるが、正確には不明である。神域は[[平戸島]]の南端近くにある[[志々伎山]]（標高347.2m）を中心としており、古くは山の頂上に上宮、中腹に中宮、麓にある漁村の宮の浦に地の宮（下宮、邊都宮）、そして宮の浦沖の沖ノ島に沖の宮がそれぞれ置かれ、別に別当寺として円満寺が設けられていた。祭神は[[日本武尊]]の子、[[仲哀天皇]]の異母弟で、[[神功皇后]]の[[三韓征伐]]に従い、その後当地に留まり西国警護を命ぜられたと伝えられる[[十城別王]]（十城別命）で、地の宮は王が設けた武器庫を転用したもの、沖の宮は王の墓所であると伝えられている。 「[[延喜式]]」神名帳において[[肥前国]]内では4社ある[[式内社]]の一つとしてその名があり、壱岐対馬を除く長崎県内では唯一であり、最も古くから知られた神社である。その後も[[松浦党]]諸家や周辺地域漁民の崇敬を広く集めた。 現在の祭祀等の中心は中宮となっている。当初志々伎山山頂下の祠にあったが、[[1559年]]（永禄2年）に中腹に、さらに[[1961年]]（昭和36年）に明治初年に[[神仏分離令]]により廃された円満寺（当社の[[別当寺]]）の跡地である現在地に移った。 旧社殿地は[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に「旧式内社志々伎神社跡」として長崎県史跡に指定されている。 ==関連項目== *[[神社一覧]] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ししきしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:肥前国の式内社]] [[Category:平戸市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{Right|{{Location map|Japan Nagasaki#Japan|label=志々伎神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=志々伎神社の位置}}}} }} '''志々伎神社'''（しじきじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[平戸市]]野子町にある神社。志志伎神社あるいは志自伎神社とも表記する。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。九州各地に志々岐・志式・志自岐として分社がある。 == 概要 == 創建年代は5世紀頃とも言われるが、正確には不明である。神域は[[平戸島]]の南端近くにある[[志々伎山]]（標高347.2[[メートル]]）を中心としており、古くは山の頂上に上宮、中腹に中宮、麓にある漁村の宮の浦に地の宮（下宮、邊都宮）、そして宮の浦沖の沖ノ島に沖の宮がそれぞれ置かれ、別に別当寺として円満寺が設けられていた。祭神は[[日本武尊]]の子、[[仲哀天皇]]の異母弟で、[[神功皇后]]の[[三韓征伐]]に従い、その後当地に留まり西国警護を命ぜられたと伝えられる[[十城別王]]（十城別命）で、地の宮は王が設けた武器庫を転用したもの、沖の宮は王の墓所であると伝えられている。 『[[延喜式]]』神名帳において[[肥前国]]内では4社ある[[式内社]]の一つとしてその名があり、壱岐・対馬地域を除く長崎県内では唯一であり、最も古くから知られた神社である。その後も[[松浦党]]諸家や周辺地域漁民の崇敬を広く集めた。 現在の祭祀等の中心は中宮となっている。当初は志々伎山山頂下の祠にあったが、[[永禄]]2年（[[1559年]]）に中腹に、さらに[[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）に明治初年に[[神仏分離令]]により廃された円満寺（当社の[[別当寺]]）の跡地である現在地に移った。 旧社殿地は[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に「旧式内社志々伎神社跡」として長崎県史跡に指定されている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 参照 === {{Reflist|2}} == 関連項目 == * [[神社一覧]] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ししきしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:肥前国の式内社]] [[Category:平戸市の歴史]] [[Category:平戸市の建築物]]

{{Right|{{Location map|Japan Nagasaki#Japan|label=志々伎神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=志々伎神社の位置}}}} }} '''志々伎神社'''（しじきじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[平戸市]]野子町にある神社。志志伎神社あるいは志自伎神社とも表記する。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。九州各地に志々岐・志式・志自岐として分社がある。規模が非常に大きく県下の式内社で最大。ここに古くから伝わる神相撲は、今日の[[相撲]]の原形といわれる[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 志々伎神社]. 角川日本地名大辞典.JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html 志々伎神社の祭礼]”. 志々伎神社-3. なすび歴史館. 2003年5月18日時点の[http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html オリジナル]{{リンク切れ|date=2022年2月}}よりアーカイブ。 == 概要 == 創建年代は5世紀頃とも言われるが、正確には不明である。神域は[[平戸島]]の南端近くにある[[志々伎山]]（標高347.2[[メートル]]）を中心としており、古くは山の頂上に上宮、中腹に中宮、麓にある漁村の宮の浦に地の宮（下宮、邊都宮）、そして宮の浦沖の沖ノ島に沖の宮がそれぞれ置かれ、別に別当寺として円満寺が設けられていた。祭神は[[日本武尊]]の子、[[仲哀天皇]]の異母弟で、[[神功皇后]]の[[三韓征伐]]に従い、その後当地に留まり西国警護を命ぜられたと伝えられる[[十城別王]]（十城別命）で、地の宮は王が設けた武器庫を転用したもの、沖の宮は王の墓所であると伝えられている。 『[[延喜式]]』神名帳において[[肥前国]]内では4社ある[[式内社]]の一つとしてその名があり、壱岐・対馬地域を除く長崎県内では唯一であり、最も古くから知られた神社である。その後も[[松浦党]]諸家や周辺地域漁民の崇敬を広く集めた。 現在の祭祀等の中心は中宮となっている。当初は志々伎山山頂下の祠にあったが、[[永禄]]2年（[[1559年]]）に中腹に、さらに[[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）に明治初年に[[神仏分離令]]により廃された円満寺（当社の[[別当寺]]）の跡地である現在地に移った。 旧社殿地は[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に「旧式内社志々伎神社跡」として長崎県史跡に指定されている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 参照 === {{Reflist|2}} == 関連項目 == * [[神社一覧]] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ししきしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:肥前国の式内社]] [[Category:平戸市の歴史]] [[Category:平戸市の建築物]]

Shikinaisha to be covered
[[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E5%BF%97%E3%80%85%E4%BC%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

{{Shinto Shrine |Name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) |Image=[[File:Shijiki-jinja, Hirado 202405-1.jpg|250px]] |Location={{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県}}{{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市}} Nogomachi 251 |Coordinates={{Wiki Coordinates Two-Level DMS|33|10|44.15|N|129|22|08.55|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王}} |Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small)
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Main Hall= |Alternate name= |Temple sites, etc.= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map={{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=place-of-worship}}
{{Right|{{Location map|Japan Nagasaki#Japan|label=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=Location of Shijiki Shrine}}}} }} '''Shijiki Shrine''' (Shijikijinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Nogomachi, {{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市}} in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県}}. It is also referred to as Shijiki Shrine or Shiji Shrine. It holds the status of a {{ill|Engi-shiki Divine Registry|de|Engi-shiki Göttliche Namensliste|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Small Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Kleine Shikinaisha}} and a {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|de|Modernes Schreinsystem|ja|近代社格制度|lt=Old Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格|lt_de=Alte Schreinsystem}} shrine, designating it as a prefectural shrine. Branch shrines exist throughout Kyushu under the names Shijiki, Shishiki, and Shiji, making it the largest among prefectural Shikinaisha. Ancient deity sumo, preserved here, is said to be the precursor to modern {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumo|ja|相撲}}.[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 志々伎神社]. 角川日本地名大辞典.JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3. Nasubi History Museum. As of May 18, 2003, the [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Original]{{リンク切れ|date=2022年2月}} archive The Jangara, performed throughout Hirado Island, also spread from this shrine. == Overview == It is said that the shrine was founded around the 5th century, though the exact date remains unknown. The sacred precinct centers on {{ill|Shijiki Mountain|de|Shijiki Berg|ja|志々伎山}} (with an elevation of 347.2 {{ill|meter|de|Meter|ja|メートル}}) near the southern tip of {{ill|Hirado Island|de|Hirado-Insel|ja|平戸島}}. In ancient times, the upper shrine was located at the mountain summit, the middle shrine on the mid-slope, and the ground shrine (also known as the lower shrine or Hentomiyashiro) was situated in the fishing village of Miyano-ura at the foot, while an offshore shrine was placed on an islet off Miyano-ura. In addition, a bettō-ji known as Enman-ji was established. The enshrined deity is the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|de|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, and the half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. He is said to have followed {{ill|Empress Jingū|de|Kaiserin Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} in the {{ill|Campaigns against the Three Han|de|Drei-Han-Feldzüge|ja|三韓征伐}}, later remaining in the area and being commissioned to guard the western regions, known as {{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王}} ({{ill|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|de|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|ja|十城別命}})). The ground shrine is said to have repurposed an armory established by the king, while the offshore shrine is believed to mark his tomb. In the Divine Name List of the {{ill|Engi-shiki|de|Engi-shiki|ja|延喜式}}, within {{ill|Hizen Province|de|Provinz Hizen|ja|肥前国}}, it is listed as one of the four {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}; it is the only shrine of its kind in Nagasaki Prefecture, excluding the Iki and Tsushima regions, and is the oldest known shrine. It has long garnered widespread reverence from the {{ill|Matsuura Faction|de|Matsuura Fraktion|ja|松浦党}} and local fishermen. The current center of worship is the middle shrine. Originally, the shrine was housed in a small structure at the foot of Shijiki Mountain’s summit, but it was relocated to the mid-slope in {{ill|Yōroku|de|Yōroku|ja|永禄}} 2 ({{ill|1559|de|1559|ja|1559年}}). Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:History of Hirado City]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirado City]] [[Category:completion tier 1]][[ja:志々伎神社]][[de:Shijiki Shrine]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E5%BF%97%E3%80%85%E4%BC%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

{{Shinto Shrine |Name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) |Image=[[File:Shijiki-jinja, Hirado 202405-1.jpg|250px]] |Location={{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県}}{{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市}} Nogomachi 251 |Coordinates={{Wiki Coordinates Two-Level DMS|33|10|44.15|N|129|22|08.55|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王}} |Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small)

Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:History of Hirado City]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirado City]] [[Category:completion tier 1]][[ja:志々伎神社]][[de:Shijiki Shrine]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E5%BF%97%E3%80%85%E4%BC%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shijiki Shrine]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine |Name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) |image=[[File:Shijiki-jinja, Hirado 202405-1.jpg|250px]] |Location={{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県}}{{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市}} Nogomachi 251 |Coordinates={{Wiki Coordinates Two-Level DMS|33|10|44.15|N|129|22|08.55|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王}} |Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small)
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Main Hall= |Alternate name= |Temple sites, etc.= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map={{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=place-of-worship}}
{{Right|{{Location map|Japan Nagasaki#Japan|label=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=Location of Shijiki Shrine}}}} }} '''Shijiki Shrine''' (Shijikijinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Nogomachi, {{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市}} in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県}}. It is also referred to as Shijiki Shrine or Shiji Shrine. It holds the status of a {{ill|Engi-shiki Divine Registry|de|Engi-shiki Göttliche Namensliste|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Small Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Kleine Shikinaisha}} and a {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|de|Modernes Schreinsystem|ja|近代社格制度|lt=Old Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格|lt_de=Alte Schreinsystem}} shrine, designating it as a prefectural shrine. Branch shrines exist throughout Kyushu under the names Shijiki, Shishiki, and Shiji, making it the largest among prefectural Shikinaisha. Ancient deity sumo, preserved here, is said to be the precursor to modern {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumo|ja|相撲}}.[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 志々伎神社]. 角川日本地名大辞典.JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3. Nasubi History Museum. As of May 18, 2003, the [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Original]{{リンク切れ|date=2022年2月}} archive The Jangara, performed throughout Hirado Island, also spread from this shrine. == Overview == It is said that the shrine was founded around the 5th century, though the exact date remains unknown. The sacred precinct centers on {{ill|Shijiki Mountain|de|Shijiki Berg|ja|志々伎山}} (with an elevation of 347.2 {{ill|meter|de|Meter|ja|メートル}}) near the southern tip of {{ill|Hirado Island|de|Hirado-Insel|ja|平戸島}}. In ancient times, the upper shrine was located at the mountain summit, the middle shrine on the mid-slope, and the ground shrine (also known as the lower shrine or Hentomiyashiro) was situated in the fishing village of Miyano-ura at the foot, while an offshore shrine was placed on an islet off Miyano-ura. In addition, a bettō-ji known as Enman-ji was established. The enshrined deity is the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|de|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, and the half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. He is said to have followed {{ill|Empress Jingū|de|Kaiserin Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} in the {{ill|Campaigns against the Three Han|de|Drei-Han-Feldzüge|ja|三韓征伐}}, later remaining in the area and being commissioned to guard the western regions, known as {{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王}} ({{ill|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|de|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|ja|十城別命}})). The ground shrine is said to have repurposed an armory established by the king, while the offshore shrine is believed to mark his tomb. In the Divine Name List of the {{ill|Engi-shiki|de|Engi-shiki|ja|延喜式}}, within {{ill|Hizen Province|de|Provinz Hizen|ja|肥前国}}, it is listed as one of the four {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}; it is the only shrine of its kind in Nagasaki Prefecture, excluding the Iki and Tsushima regions, and is the oldest known shrine. It has long garnered widespread reverence from the {{ill|Matsuura Faction|de|Matsuura Fraktion|ja|松浦党}} and local fishermen. The current center of worship is the middle shrine. Originally, the shrine was housed in a small structure at the foot of Shijiki Mountain’s summit, but it was relocated to the mid-slope in {{ill|Yōroku|de|Yōroku|ja|永禄}} 2 ({{ill|1559|de|1559|ja|1559年}}). Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:History of Hirado City]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirado City]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:志々伎神社]] [[de:Shijiki Shrine]] [[ja:%E5%BF%97%E3%80%85%E4%BC%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Shijiki Shrine]]

As of May 18, 2003, the [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Original]{{リンク切れ|date=2022年2月}} archive The Jangara, performed throughout Hirado Island, also spread from this shrine. == Overview == It is said that the shrine was founded around the 5th century, though the exact date remains unknown. The sacred precinct centers on {{ill|Shijiki Mountain|de|Shijiki Berg|ja|志々伎山|12=draft}} (with an elevation of 347.2 {{ill|meter|de|Meter|ja|メートル|12=draft}}) near the southern tip of {{ill|Hirado Island|de|Hirado-Insel|ja|平戸島|12=draft}}. In ancient times, the upper shrine was located at the mountain summit, the middle shrine on the mid-slope, and the ground shrine (also known as the lower shrine or Hentomiyashiro) was situated in the fishing village of Miyano-ura at the foot, while an offshore shrine was placed on an islet off Miyano-ura. In addition, a bettō-ji known as Enman-ji was established. The enshrined deity is the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|de|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|12=draft}}, and the half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}. He is said to have followed {{ill|Empress Jingū|de|Kaiserin Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Campaigns against the Three Han|de|Drei-Han-Feldzüge|ja|三韓征伐|12=draft}}, later remaining in the area and being commissioned to guard the western regions, known as {{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|12=draft}} ({{ill|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|de|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|ja|十城別命|12=draft}})). The ground shrine is said to have repurposed an armory established by the king, while the offshore shrine is believed to mark his tomb. In the Divine Name List of the {{ill|Engi-shiki|de|Engi-shiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}, within {{ill|Hizen Province|de|Provinz Hizen|ja|肥前国|12=draft}}, it is listed as one of the four {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}; it is the only shrine of its kind in Nagasaki Prefecture, excluding the Iki and Tsushima regions, and is the oldest known shrine. It has long garnered widespread reverence from the {{ill|Matsuura Faction|de|Matsuura Fraktion|ja|松浦党|12=draft}} and local fishermen. The current center of worship is the middle shrine. Originally, the shrine was housed in a small structure at the foot of Shijiki Mountain’s summit, but it was relocated to the mid-slope in {{ill|Yōroku|de|Yōroku|ja|永禄|12=draft}} 2 ({{ill|1559|de|1559|ja|1559年|12=draft}}). Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年|12=draft}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年|12=draft}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧|12=draft}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:History of Hirado City]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirado City]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:志々伎神社]] [[de:Shijiki Shrine]] [[ja:%E5%BF%97%E3%80%85%E4%BC%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Shijiki Shrine]]

Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年|12=draft}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年|12=draft}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧|12=draft}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:History of Hirado City]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirado City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:志々伎神社]] [[de:Shijiki Shrine]] [[ja:%E5%BF%97%E3%80%85%E4%BC%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Shijiki Shrine]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine |Name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) |image=[[File:Shijiki-jinja, Hirado 202405-1.jpg|250px]] |Location={{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|12=draft}}{{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|12=draft}} Nogomachi 251 |Coordinates={{Wiki Coordinates Two-Level DMS|33|10|44.15|N|129|22|08.55|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|12=draft}} |Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small)
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Main Hall= |Alternate name= |Temple sites, etc.= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map={{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=place-of-worship}}
{{Right|{{Location map|Japan Nagasaki#Japan|label=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=Location of Shijiki Shrine}}}} }} '''Shijiki Shrine''' (Shijikijinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Nogomachi, {{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|12=draft}}. It is also referred to as Shijiki Shrine or Shiji Shrine. It holds the status of a {{ill|Engi-shiki Divine Registry|de|Engi-shiki Göttliche Namensliste|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Small Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Kleine Shikinaisha|12=draft}} and a {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|de|Modernes Schreinsystem|ja|近代社格制度|lt=Old Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格|lt_de=Alte Schreinsystem|12=draft}} shrine, designating it as a prefectural shrine. Branch shrines exist throughout Kyushu under the names Shijiki, Shishiki, and Shiji, making it the largest among prefectural Shikinaisha. Ancient deity sumo, preserved here, is said to be the precursor to modern {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumo|ja|相撲|12=draft}}.[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 志々伎神社]. 角川日本地名大辞典.JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3. Nasubi History Museum. As of May 18, 2003, the [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Original]{{リンク切れ|date=2022年2月}} archive The Jangara, performed throughout Hirado Island, also spread from this shrine. == Overview == It is said that the shrine was founded around the 5th century, though the exact date remains unknown. The sacred precinct centers on {{ill|Shijiki Mountain|de|Shijiki Berg|ja|志々伎山|12=draft}} (with an elevation of 347.2 {{ill|meter|de|Meter|ja|メートル|12=draft}}) near the southern tip of {{ill|Hirado Island|de|Hirado-Insel|ja|平戸島|12=draft}}. In ancient times, the upper shrine was located at the mountain summit, the middle shrine on the mid-slope, and the ground shrine (also known as the lower shrine or Hentomiyashiro) was situated in the fishing village of Miyano-ura at the foot, while an offshore shrine was placed on an islet off Miyano-ura. In addition, a bettō-ji known as Enman-ji was established. The enshrined deity is the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|de|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|12=draft}}, and the half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}. He is said to have followed {{ill|Empress Jingū|de|Kaiserin Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Campaigns against the Three Han|de|Drei-Han-Feldzüge|ja|三韓征伐|12=draft}}, later remaining in the area and being commissioned to guard the western regions, known as {{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|12=draft}} ({{ill|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|de|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|ja|十城別命|12=draft}})). The ground shrine is said to have repurposed an armory established by the king, while the offshore shrine is believed to mark his tomb. In the Divine Name List of the {{ill|Engi-shiki|de|Engi-shiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}, within {{ill|Hizen Province|de|Provinz Hizen|ja|肥前国|12=draft}}, it is listed as one of the four {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}}; it is the only shrine of its kind in Nagasaki Prefecture, excluding the Iki and Tsushima regions, and is the oldest known shrine. It has long garnered widespread reverence from the {{ill|Matsuura Faction|de|Matsuura Fraktion|ja|松浦党|12=draft}} and local fishermen. The current center of worship is the middle shrine. Originally, the shrine was housed in a small structure at the foot of Shijiki Mountain’s summit, but it was relocated to the mid-slope in {{ill|Yōroku|de|Yōroku|ja|永禄|12=draft}} 2 ({{ill|1559|de|1559|ja|1559年|12=draft}}). Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年|12=draft}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年|12=draft}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧|12=draft}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:History of Hirado City]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirado City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:志々伎神社]] [[de:Shijiki Shrine]] [[ja:%E5%BF%97%E3%80%85%E4%BC%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Shijiki Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=志々伎神社 |画像=[[File:Shijiki-jinja, Hirado 202405-1.jpg|250px]] |所在地=[[長崎県]][[平戸市]]野子町251番地 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|10|44.15|N|129|22|08.55|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=志々伎神社}} |祭神=十城別王 |社格=式内社（小）
旧県社 |創建=不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=place-of-worship}}
{{Right|{{Location map|Japan Nagasaki#Japan|label=志々伎神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=志々伎神社の位置}}}} }} '''志々伎神社'''（しじきじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[平戸市]]野子町にある神社。志志伎神社あるいは志自伎神社とも表記する。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。九州各地に志々岐・志式・志自岐として分社がある。規模が大きく県下の式内社で最大。ここに古くから伝わる神相撲は、今日の[[相撲]]の原形といわれる[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 志々伎神社]. 角川日本地名大辞典.JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html 志々伎神社の祭礼]”. 志々伎神社-3. なすび歴史館. 2003年5月18日時点の[http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html オリジナル]{{リンク切れ|date=2022年2月}}よりアーカイブ。平戸全島で行われる[[平戸のジャンガラ|ジャンガラ]]も当社から広まったものである。 == 概要 == 創建年代は5世紀頃とも言われるが、正確には不明である。神域は[[平戸島]]の南端近くにある[[志々伎山]]（標高347.2[[メートル]]）を中心としており、古くは山の頂上に上宮、中腹に中宮、麓にある漁村の宮の浦に地の宮（下宮、邊都宮）、そして宮の浦沖の沖ノ島に沖の宮がそれぞれ置かれ、別に別当寺として円満寺が設けられていた。祭神は[[日本武尊]]の子、[[仲哀天皇]]の異母弟で、[[神功皇后]]の[[三韓征伐]]に従い、その後当地に留まり西国警護を命ぜられたと伝えられる[[十城別王]]（十城別命）で、地の宮は王が設けた武器庫を転用したもの、沖の宮は王の墓所であると伝えられている。 『[[延喜式]]』神名帳において[[肥前国]]内では4社ある[[式内社]]の一つとしてその名があり、壱岐・対馬地域を除く長崎県内では唯一であり、最も古くから知られた神社である。その後も[[松浦党]]諸家や周辺地域漁民の崇敬を広く集めた。 現在の祭祀等の中心は中宮となっている。当初は志々伎山山頂下の祠にあったが、[[永禄]]2年（[[1559年]]）に中腹に、さらに[[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）に明治初年に[[神仏分離令]]により廃された円満寺（当社の[[別当寺]]）の跡地である現在地に移った。 旧社殿地は[[1974年]]（昭和49年）に「旧式内社志々伎神社跡」として長崎県史跡に指定されている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 参照 === {{Reflist|2}} == 関連項目 == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * [[神社一覧]] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ししきしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:肥前国の式内社]] [[Category:平戸市の歴史]] [[Category:平戸市の建築物]]

Shijiki Shrine-3. Nasubi History Museum. As of May 18, 2003, the [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Original]{{リンク切れ|date=2022年2月}} archive The Jangara, performed throughout Hirado Island, also spread from this shrine. == Overview == It is said that the shrine was founded around the 5th century, though the exact date remains unknown. The sacred precinct centers on {{ill|Shijiki Mountain|de|Shijiki Berg|ja|志々伎山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijiki Mountain|qq=}} (with an elevation of 347.2 {{ill|meter|de|Meter|ja|メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter|qq=}}) near the southern tip of {{ill|Hirado Island|de|Hirado-Insel|ja|平戸島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado Island|qq=}}. In ancient times, the upper shrine was located at the mountain summit, the middle shrine on the mid-slope, and the ground shrine (also known as the lower shrine or Hentomiyashiro) was situated in the fishing village of Miyano-ura at the foot, while an offshore shrine was placed on an islet off Miyano-ura. In addition, a bettō-ji known as Enman-ji was established. The enshrined deity is the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|de|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, and the half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}. He is said to have followed {{ill|Empress Jingū|de|Kaiserin Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} in the {{ill|Campaigns against the Three Han|de|Drei-Han-Feldzüge|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaigns against the Three Han|qq=}}, later remaining in the area and being commissioned to guard the western regions, known as {{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} ({{ill|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|de|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|ja|十城別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|qq=}})). The ground shrine is said to have repurposed an armory established by the king, while the offshore shrine is believed to mark his tomb. In the Divine Name List of the {{ill|Engi-shiki|de|Engi-shiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki|qq=}}, within {{ill|Hizen Province|de|Provinz Hizen|ja|肥前国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen Province|qq=}}, it is listed as one of the four {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}; it is the only shrine of its kind in Nagasaki Prefecture, excluding the Iki and Tsushima regions, and is the oldest known shrine. It has long garnered widespread reverence from the {{ill|Matsuura Faction|de|Matsuura Fraktion|ja|松浦党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuura Faction|qq=}} and local fishermen. The current center of worship is the middle shrine. Originally, the shrine was housed in a small structure at the foot of Shijiki Mountain’s summit, but it was relocated to the mid-slope in {{ill|Yōroku|de|Yōroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōroku|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1559|de|1559|ja|1559年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1559|qq=}}). Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1961|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|qq=}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974|qq=}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:History of Hirado City]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirado City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:志々伎神社]] [[de:Shijiki Shrine]] [[ja:%E5%BF%97%E3%80%85%E4%BC%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Shijiki Shrine]] ==Merged second translation== == Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) == '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志々伎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Noko Town|ja|野子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noko Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirado, Nagasaki|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado, Nagasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also written as '''Shishiki Shrine''' or '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志志伎神社 or 志自伎神社). It is listed as a '''small-scale''' {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and under the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', it held the status of a '''former prefectural shrine'''. Several branch shrines bearing the name '''Shijiki''', '''Shishiki''', or '''Shijiki Shrine''' exist throughout Kyushu. As the '''largest of Nagasaki's Shikinaisha''', it has played a significant historical role. The '''sacred sumo ritual''' practiced here is believed to be an early form of '''sumo wrestling'''[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 Shijiki Shrine]. Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia, JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3, Nasubi Historical Archive. Archived from [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html the original] on May 18, 2003.. The '''Hirado Jangara Dance''' (平戸のジャンガラ), a local folk performance seen across '''Hirado Island''', is said to have originated from this shrine. == Overview == The founding date of '''Shijiki Shrine''' is uncertain, though some sources suggest it dates back to the '''5th century'''. The shrine’s '''sacred domain''' is centered on '''Mount Shijiki''' (志々伎山), located near the '''southern tip of Hirado Island'''. Traditionally, the shrine was divided into four main sanctuaries: * '''Upper Shrine (Kami-no-Miya)''': Located at the '''summit''' of Mount Shijiki. * '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''': Located '''midway up the mountain'''. * '''Lower Shrine (Chi-no-Miya)''': Located at '''Miyanoura''', a coastal fishing village. * '''Offshore Shrine (Oki-no-Miya)''': Located on '''Okinoshima''', an island off the coast. Additionally, the '''Enman-ji Temple''' (円満寺) historically served as the shrine’s '''bettō-ji''' (shrine-temple complex before the Meiji-era Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''deity enshrined''' here is '''Tokowake-no-Ō''' (十城別王), also known as '''Tokowake-no-Mikoto''' (十城別命), the '''son of Yamato Takeru''' and the '''half-brother of Emperor Chūai'''. He is believed to have '''accompanied Empress Jingū on her conquest of Korea''' and was later '''assigned to defend Western Japan'''. According to legend: * '''Chi-no-Miya''' (Lower Shrine) was originally a '''military weapons storehouse''' established by Tokowake-no-Ō. * '''Oki-no-Miya''' (Offshore Shrine) is said to '''house his tomb'''. Shijiki Shrine is listed in the '''Engishiki''' (延喜式) as '''one of only four Shikinaisha''' in '''Hizen Province'''. Excluding the '''Iki and Tsushima regions''', it is the '''only Engishiki shrine in modern Nagasaki Prefecture'''. Historically, it attracted strong patronage from the '''Matsura clan''' (松浦党) and the surrounding '''maritime communities'''. Today, the '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''' is the primary place of worship. The shrine originally '''stood at the mountain’s peak''', but in '''1559 (Eiroku 2)''', it was moved '''midway down the mountain'''. In '''1961 (Shōwa 36)''', it was '''relocated again''' to the '''former site of Enman-ji Temple''', which had been abolished following the '''Meiji-era Shinbutsu Bunri''' (Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''original shrine site''' was designated a '''Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site''' in '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' as the '''"Former Site of the Shikinai Shrine Shijiki Shrine"'''. == References == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === Citations === {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shikizuki Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:History of Hirado, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hirado, Nagasaki]] }} [[ja:%E5%BF%97%E3%80%85%E4%BC%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Shikizuki Shrine]] [[en:Shikizuki Shrine]] [[ja:志々伎神社]] [[Category:Merged pages]]

{{Shinto Shrine |Name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) |image=[[File:Shijiki-jinja, Hirado 202405-1.jpg|250px]] |Location={{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City|qq=}} Nogomachi 251 |Coordinates={{Wiki Coordinates Two-Level DMS|33|10|44.15|N|129|22|08.55|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} |Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small)
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Main Hall= |Alternate name= |Temple sites, etc.= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map={{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=place-of-worship}}
{{Right|{{Location map|Japan Nagasaki#Japan|label=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=Location of Shijiki Shrine}}}} }} '''Shijiki Shrine''' (Shijikijinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Nogomachi, {{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City|qq=}} in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also referred to as Shijiki Shrine or Shiji Shrine. It holds the status of a {{ill|Engi-shiki Divine Registry|de|Engi-shiki Göttliche Namensliste|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Small Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Kleine Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki Divine Registry|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and a {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|de|Modernes Schreinsystem|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Old Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格|lt_de=Alte Schreinsystem|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} shrine, designating it as a prefectural shrine. Branch shrines exist throughout Kyushu under the names Shijiki, Shishiki, and Shiji, making it the largest among prefectural Shikinaisha. Ancient deity sumo, preserved here, is said to be the precursor to modern {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumo|ja|相撲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumo|qq=}}.[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 志々伎神社]. 角川日本地名大辞典.JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3. Nasubi History Museum. As of May 18, 2003, the [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Original]{{リンク切れ|date=2022年2月}} archive The Jangara, performed throughout Hirado Island, also spread from this shrine. == Overview == It is said that the shrine was founded around the 5th century, though the exact date remains unknown. The sacred precinct centers on {{ill|Shijiki Mountain|de|Shijiki Berg|ja|志々伎山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijiki Mountain|qq=}} (with an elevation of 347.2 {{ill|meter|de|Meter|ja|メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter|qq=}}) near the southern tip of {{ill|Hirado Island|de|Hirado-Insel|ja|平戸島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado Island|qq=}}. In addition, a bettō-ji known as Enman-ji was established. He is said to have followed {{ill|Empress Jingū|de|Kaiserin Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} in the {{ill|Campaigns against the Three Han|de|Drei-Han-Feldzüge|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaigns against the Three Han|qq=}}, later remaining in the area and being commissioned to guard the western regions, known as {{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} ({{ill|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|de|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|ja|十城別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|qq=}})). The ground shrine is said to have repurposed an armory established by the king, while the offshore shrine is believed to mark his tomb. In the Divine Name List of the {{ill|Engi-shiki|de|Engi-shiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki|qq=}}, within {{ill|Hizen Province|de|Provinz Hizen|ja|肥前国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen Province|qq=}}, it is listed as one of the four {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}; it is the only shrine of its kind in Nagasaki Prefecture, excluding the Iki and Tsushima regions, and is the oldest known shrine. It has long garnered widespread reverence from the {{ill|Matsuura Faction|de|Matsuura Fraktion|ja|松浦党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuura Faction|qq=}} and local fishermen. The current center of worship is the middle shrine. Originally, the shrine was housed in a small structure at the foot of Shijiki Mountain’s summit, but it was relocated to the mid-slope in {{ill|Yōroku|de|Yōroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōroku|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1559|de|1559|ja|1559年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1559|qq=}}). Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1961|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|qq=}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974|qq=}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:History of Hirado City]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirado City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:志々伎神社]] [[de:Shijiki Shrine]] [[ja:%E5%BF%97%E3%80%85%E4%BC%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Shijiki Shrine]] ==Merged second translation== == Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) == '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志々伎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Noko Town|ja|野子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noko Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirado, Nagasaki|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado, Nagasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. The '''Hirado Jangara Dance''' (平戸のジャンガラ), a local folk performance seen across '''Hirado Island''', is said to have originated from this shrine. == Overview == The founding date of '''Shijiki Shrine''' is uncertain, though some sources suggest it dates back to the '''5th century'''. The shrine’s '''sacred domain''' is centered on '''Mount Shijiki''' (志々伎山), located near the '''southern tip of Hirado Island'''. Traditionally, the shrine was divided into four main sanctuaries: * '''Upper Shrine (Kami-no-Miya)''': Located at the '''summit''' of Mount Shijiki. * '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''': Located '''midway up the mountain'''. * '''Lower Shrine (Chi-no-Miya)''': Located at '''Miyanoura''', a coastal fishing village. * '''Offshore Shrine (Oki-no-Miya)''': Located on '''Okinoshima''', an island off the coast. Additionally, the '''Enman-ji Temple''' (円満寺) historically served as the shrine’s '''bettō-ji''' (shrine-temple complex before the Meiji-era Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''deity enshrined''' here is '''Tokowake-no-Ō''' (十城別王), also known as '''Tokowake-no-Mikoto''' (十城別命), the '''son of Yamato Takeru''' and the '''half-brother of Emperor Chūai'''. He is believed to have '''accompanied Empress Jingū on her conquest of Korea''' and was later '''assigned to defend Western Japan'''. According to legend: * '''Chi-no-Miya''' (Lower Shrine) was originally a '''military weapons storehouse''' established by Tokowake-no-Ō. * '''Oki-no-Miya''' (Offshore Shrine) is said to '''house his tomb'''. Shijiki Shrine is listed in the '''Engishiki''' (延喜式) as '''one of only four Shikinaisha''' in '''Hizen Province'''. Excluding the '''Iki and Tsushima regions''', it is the '''only Engishiki shrine in modern Nagasaki Prefecture'''. Historically, it attracted strong patronage from the '''Matsura clan''' (松浦党) and the surrounding '''maritime communities'''. Today, the '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''' is the primary place of worship. The shrine originally '''stood at the mountain’s peak''', but in '''1559 (Eiroku 2)''', it was moved '''midway down the mountain'''. In '''1961 (Shōwa 36)''', it was '''relocated again''' to the '''former site of Enman-ji Temple''', which had been abolished following the '''Meiji-era Shinbutsu Bunri''' (Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''original shrine site''' was designated a '''Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site''' in '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' as the '''"Former Site of the Shikinai Shrine Shijiki Shrine"'''. == References == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === Citations === {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shikizuki Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:History of Hirado, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hirado, Nagasaki]] }} [[ja:%E5%BF%97%E3%80%85%E4%BC%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Shikizuki Shrine]] [[en:Shikizuki Shrine]] [[ja:志々伎神社]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shijiki Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine |Name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) |image=[[File:Shijiki-jinja, Hirado 202405-1.jpg|250px]] |Location={{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City|qq=}} Nogomachi 251 |Coordinates={{Wiki Coordinates Two-Level DMS|33|10|44.15|N|129|22|08.55|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} |Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small)
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Main Hall= |Alternate name= |Temple sites, etc.= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map={{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=place-of-worship}}
{{Right|{{Location map|Japan Nagasaki#Japan|label=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=Location of Shijiki Shrine}}}} }} '''Shijiki Shrine''' (Shijikijinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Nogomachi, {{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City|qq=}} in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also referred to as Shijiki Shrine or Shiji Shrine. It holds the status of a {{ill|Engi-shiki Divine Registry|de|Engi-shiki Göttliche Namensliste|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Small Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Kleine Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki Divine Registry|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and a {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|de|Modernes Schreinsystem|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Old Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格|lt_de=Alte Schreinsystem|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} shrine, designating it as a prefectural shrine. Branch shrines exist throughout Kyushu under the names Shijiki, Shishiki, and Shiji, making it the largest among prefectural Shikinaisha. Ancient deity sumo, preserved here, is said to be the precursor to modern {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumo|ja|相撲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumo|qq=}}.[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 志々伎神社]. 角川日本地名大辞典.JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3. Nasubi History Museum. As of May 18, 2003, the [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Original]{{リンク切れ|date=2022年2月}} archive The Jangara, performed throughout Hirado Island, also spread from this shrine. == Overview == It is said that the shrine was founded around the 5th century, though the exact date remains unknown. The sacred precinct centers on {{ill|Shijiki Mountain|de|Shijiki Berg|ja|志々伎山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijiki Mountain|qq=}} (with an elevation of 347.2 {{ill|meter|de|Meter|ja|メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter|qq=}}) near the southern tip of {{ill|Hirado Island|de|Hirado-Insel|ja|平戸島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado Island|qq=}}. In ancient times, the upper shrine was located at the mountain summit, the middle shrine on the mid-slope, and the ground shrine (also known as the lower shrine or Hentomiyashiro) was situated in the fishing village of Miyano-ura at the foot, while an offshore shrine was placed on an islet off Miyano-ura. In addition, a bettō-ji known as Enman-ji was established. The enshrined deity is the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|de|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, and the half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}. He is said to have followed {{ill|Empress Jingū|de|Kaiserin Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} in the {{ill|Campaigns against the Three Han|de|Drei-Han-Feldzüge|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaigns against the Three Han|qq=}}, later remaining in the area and being commissioned to guard the western regions, known as {{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} ({{ill|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|de|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|ja|十城別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|qq=}})). The ground shrine is said to have repurposed an armory established by the king, while the offshore shrine is believed to mark his tomb. In the Divine Name List of the {{ill|Engi-shiki|de|Engi-shiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki|qq=}}, within {{ill|Hizen Province|de|Provinz Hizen|ja|肥前国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen Province|qq=}}, it is listed as one of the four {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}; it is the only shrine of its kind in Nagasaki Prefecture, excluding the Iki and Tsushima regions, and is the oldest known shrine. It has long garnered widespread reverence from the {{ill|Matsuura Faction|de|Matsuura Fraktion|ja|松浦党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuura Faction|qq=}} and local fishermen. The current center of worship is the middle shrine. Originally, the shrine was housed in a small structure at the foot of Shijiki Mountain’s summit, but it was relocated to the mid-slope in {{ill|Yōroku|de|Yōroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōroku|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1559|de|1559|ja|1559年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1559|qq=}}). Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1961|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|qq=}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974|qq=}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:History of Hirado City]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirado City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:志々伎神社]] [[de:Shijiki Shrine]] [[ja:%E5%BF%97%E3%80%85%E4%BC%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Shijiki Shrine]] ==Merged second translation== == Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) == '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志々伎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Noko Town|ja|野子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noko Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirado, Nagasaki|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado, Nagasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also written as '''Shishiki Shrine''' or '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志志伎神社 or 志自伎神社). It is listed as a '''small-scale''' {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and under the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', it held the status of a '''former prefectural shrine'''. Several branch shrines bearing the name '''Shijiki''', '''Shishiki''', or '''Shijiki Shrine''' exist throughout Kyushu. As the '''largest of Nagasaki's Shikinaisha''', it has played a significant historical role. The '''sacred sumo ritual''' practiced here is believed to be an early form of '''sumo wrestling'''[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 Shijiki Shrine]. Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia, JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3, Nasubi Historical Archive. Archived from [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html the original] on May 18, 2003.. The '''Hirado Jangara Dance''' (平戸のジャンガラ), a local folk performance seen across '''Hirado Island''', is said to have originated from this shrine. == Overview == The founding date of '''Shijiki Shrine''' is uncertain, though some sources suggest it dates back to the '''5th century'''. The shrine’s '''sacred domain''' is centered on '''Mount Shijiki''' (志々伎山), located near the '''southern tip of Hirado Island'''. Traditionally, the shrine was divided into four main sanctuaries: * '''Upper Shrine (Kami-no-Miya)''': Located at the '''summit''' of Mount Shijiki. * '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''': Located '''midway up the mountain'''. * '''Lower Shrine (Chi-no-Miya)''': Located at '''Miyanoura''', a coastal fishing village. * '''Offshore Shrine (Oki-no-Miya)''': Located on '''Okinoshima''', an island off the coast. Additionally, the '''Enman-ji Temple''' (円満寺) historically served as the shrine’s '''bettō-ji''' (shrine-temple complex before the Meiji-era Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''deity enshrined''' here is '''Tokowake-no-Ō''' (十城別王), also known as '''Tokowake-no-Mikoto''' (十城別命), the '''son of Yamato Takeru''' and the '''half-brother of Emperor Chūai'''. He is believed to have '''accompanied Empress Jingū on her conquest of Korea''' and was later '''assigned to defend Western Japan'''. According to legend: * '''Chi-no-Miya''' (Lower Shrine) was originally a '''military weapons storehouse''' established by Tokowake-no-Ō. * '''Oki-no-Miya''' (Offshore Shrine) is said to '''house his tomb'''. Shijiki Shrine is listed in the '''Engishiki''' (延喜式) as '''one of only four Shikinaisha''' in '''Hizen Province'''. Excluding the '''Iki and Tsushima regions''', it is the '''only Engishiki shrine in modern Nagasaki Prefecture'''. Historically, it attracted strong patronage from the '''Matsura clan''' (松浦党) and the surrounding '''maritime communities'''. Today, the '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''' is the primary place of worship. The shrine originally '''stood at the mountain’s peak''', but in '''1559 (Eiroku 2)''', it was moved '''midway down the mountain'''. In '''1961 (Shōwa 36)''', it was '''relocated again''' to the '''former site of Enman-ji Temple''', which had been abolished following the '''Meiji-era Shinbutsu Bunri''' (Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''original shrine site''' was designated a '''Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site''' in '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' as the '''"Former Site of the Shikinai Shrine Shijiki Shrine"'''. == References == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === Citations === {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shikizuki Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:History of Hirado, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hirado, Nagasaki]] }} [[ja:%E5%BF%97%E3%80%85%E4%BC%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Shikizuki Shrine]] [[en:Shikizuki Shrine]] [[ja:志々伎神社]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shijiki Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

In ancient times, the upper shrine was located at the mountain summit, the middle shrine on the mid-slope, and the ground shrine (also known as the lower shrine or Hentomiyashiro) was situated in the fishing village of Miyano-ura at the foot, while an offshore shrine was placed on an islet off Miyano-ura. In addition, a bettō-ji known as Enman-ji was established. The enshrined deity is the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|de|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, and the half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}. He is said to have followed {{ill|Empress Jingū|de|Kaiserin Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} in the {{ill|Campaigns against the Three Han|de|Drei-Han-Feldzüge|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaigns against the Three Han|qq=}}, later remaining in the area and being commissioned to guard the western regions, known as {{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} ({{ill|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|de|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|ja|十城別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|qq=}})). The ground shrine is said to have repurposed an armory established by the king, while the offshore shrine is believed to mark his tomb. In the Divine Name List of the {{ill|Engi-shiki|de|Engi-shiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki|qq=}}, within {{ill|Hizen Province|de|Provinz Hizen|ja|肥前国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen Province|qq=}}, it is listed as one of the four {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}; it is the only shrine of its kind in Nagasaki Prefecture, excluding the Iki and Tsushima regions, and is the oldest known shrine. It has long garnered widespread reverence from the {{ill|Matsuura Faction|de|Matsuura Fraktion|ja|松浦党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuura Faction|qq=}} and local fishermen. The current center of worship is the middle shrine. Originally, the shrine was housed in a small structure at the foot of Shijiki Mountain’s summit, but it was relocated to the mid-slope in {{ill|Yōroku|de|Yōroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōroku|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1559|de|1559|ja|1559年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1559|qq=}}). Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1961|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|qq=}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974|qq=}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:History of Hirado City]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirado City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:志々伎神社]] [[de:Shijiki Shrine]] [[ja:%E5%BF%97%E3%80%85%E4%BC%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Shijiki Shrine]] ==Merged second translation== == Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) == '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志々伎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Noko Town|ja|野子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noko Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirado, Nagasaki|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado, Nagasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also written as '''Shishiki Shrine''' or '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志志伎神社 or 志自伎神社). It is listed as a '''small-scale''' {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and under the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', it held the status of a '''former prefectural shrine'''. Several branch shrines bearing the name '''Shijiki''', '''Shishiki''', or '''Shijiki Shrine''' exist throughout Kyushu. As the '''largest of Nagasaki's Shikinaisha''', it has played a significant historical role. The '''sacred sumo ritual''' practiced here is believed to be an early form of '''sumo wrestling'''[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 Shijiki Shrine]. Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia, JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3, Nasubi Historical Archive. Archived from [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html the original] on May 18, 2003.. The '''Hirado Jangara Dance''' (平戸のジャンガラ), a local folk performance seen across '''Hirado Island''', is said to have originated from this shrine. == Overview == The founding date of '''Shijiki Shrine''' is uncertain, though some sources suggest it dates back to the '''5th century'''. The shrine’s '''sacred domain''' is centered on '''Mount Shijiki''' (志々伎山), located near the '''southern tip of Hirado Island'''. Traditionally, the shrine was divided into four main sanctuaries: * '''Upper Shrine (Kami-no-Miya)''': Located at the '''summit''' of Mount Shijiki. * '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''': Located '''midway up the mountain'''. * '''Lower Shrine (Chi-no-Miya)''': Located at '''Miyanoura''', a coastal fishing village. * '''Offshore Shrine (Oki-no-Miya)''': Located on '''Okinoshima''', an island off the coast. Additionally, the '''Enman-ji Temple''' (円満寺) historically served as the shrine’s '''bettō-ji''' (shrine-temple complex before the Meiji-era Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''deity enshrined''' here is '''Tokowake-no-Ō''' (十城別王), also known as '''Tokowake-no-Mikoto''' (十城別命), the '''son of Yamato Takeru''' and the '''half-brother of Emperor Chūai'''. He is believed to have '''accompanied Empress Jingū on her conquest of Korea''' and was later '''assigned to defend Western Japan'''. According to legend: * '''Chi-no-Miya''' (Lower Shrine) was originally a '''military weapons storehouse''' established by Tokowake-no-Ō. * '''Oki-no-Miya''' (Offshore Shrine) is said to '''house his tomb'''. Shijiki Shrine is listed in the '''Engishiki''' (延喜式) as '''one of only four Shikinaisha''' in '''Hizen Province'''. Excluding the '''Iki and Tsushima regions''', it is the '''only Engishiki shrine in modern Nagasaki Prefecture'''. Historically, it attracted strong patronage from the '''Matsura clan''' (松浦党) and the surrounding '''maritime communities'''. Today, the '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''' is the primary place of worship. The shrine originally '''stood at the mountain’s peak''', but in '''1559 (Eiroku 2)''', it was moved '''midway down the mountain'''. In '''1961 (Shōwa 36)''', it was '''relocated again''' to the '''former site of Enman-ji Temple''', which had been abolished following the '''Meiji-era Shinbutsu Bunri''' (Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''original shrine site''' was designated a '''Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site''' in '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' as the '''"Former Site of the Shikinai Shrine Shijiki Shrine"'''. == References == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === Citations === {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hizen Province]] [[Category:History of Hirado, Nagasaki]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hirado, Nagasaki]] }} [[ja:%E5%BF%97%E3%80%85%E4%BC%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Shikizuki Shrine]] [[en:Shikizuki Shrine]] [[ja:志々伎神社]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shijiki Shrine]][[Category:qq]]
