revision.text:
'''Shijiki Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Hizen Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Matsura-gun|en|Matsura-gun|ja|松浦郡|ko|마쓰라군|zh|松浦郡|lt=Matsura district|WD=Q5973791}} || {{ill|Shijiki Shrine|lt=Shijiki Shrine|ja|志々伎神社|commons|Category:Shijiki-jinja|WD=Q11490808}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|33.178307|129.369101|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183317 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11490808}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Shinto Shrine |Name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) |image=[[File:Shijiki-jinja, Hirado 202405-1.jpg|250px]] |Location={{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City|qq=}} Nogomachi 251 |Coordinates={{Wiki Coordinates Two-Level DMS|33|10|44.15|N|129|22|08.55|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} |Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small)
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Main Hall= |Alternate name= |Temple sites, etc.= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map={{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=place-of-worship}}
{{Right|{{Location map|Japan Nagasaki#Japan|label=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=Location of Shijiki Shrine}}}} }} '''Shijiki Shrine''' (Shijikijinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Nogomachi, {{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City|qq=}} in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also referred to as Shijiki Shrine or Shiji Shrine. It holds the status of a {{ill|Engi-shiki Divine Registry|de|Engi-shiki Göttliche Namensliste|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Small Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Kleine Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki Divine Registry|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and a {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|de|Modernes Schreinsystem|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Old Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格|lt_de=Alte Schreinsystem|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} shrine, designating it as a prefectural shrine. Branch shrines exist throughout Kyushu under the names Shijiki, Shishiki, and Shiji, making it the largest among prefectural Shikinaisha. Ancient deity sumo, preserved here, is said to be the precursor to modern {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumo|ja|相撲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumo|qq=}}.[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 志々伎神社]. 角川日本地名大辞典.JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3. Nasubi History Museum. As of May 18, 2003, the [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Original]{{リンク切れ|date=2022年2月}} archive The Jangara, performed throughout Hirado Island, also spread from this shrine. == Overview == It is said that the shrine was founded around the 5th century, though the exact date remains unknown. The sacred precinct centers on {{ill|Shijiki Mountain|de|Shijiki Berg|ja|志々伎山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijiki Mountain|qq=}} (with an elevation of 347.2 {{ill|meter|de|Meter|ja|メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter|qq=}}) near the southern tip of {{ill|Hirado Island|de|Hirado-Insel|ja|平戸島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado Island|qq=}}. In ancient times, the upper shrine was located at the mountain summit, the middle shrine on the mid-slope, and the ground shrine (also known as the lower shrine or Hentomiyashiro) was situated in the fishing village of Miyano-ura at the foot, while an offshore shrine was placed on an islet off Miyano-ura. In addition, a bettō-ji known as Enman-ji was established. The enshrined deity is the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|de|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, and the half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}. He is said to have followed {{ill|Empress Jingū|de|Kaiserin Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} in the {{ill|Campaigns against the Three Han|de|Drei-Han-Feldzüge|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaigns against the Three Han|qq=}}, later remaining in the area and being commissioned to guard the western regions, known as {{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} ({{ill|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|de|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|ja|十城別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|qq=}})). The ground shrine is said to have repurposed an armory established by the king, while the offshore shrine is believed to mark his tomb. In the Divine Name List of the {{ill|Engi-shiki|de|Engi-shiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki|qq=}}, within {{ill|Hizen Province|de|Provinz Hizen|ja|肥前国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen Province|qq=}}, it is listed as one of the four {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}; it is the only shrine of its kind in Nagasaki Prefecture, excluding the Iki and Tsushima regions, and is the oldest known shrine. It has long garnered widespread reverence from the {{ill|Matsuura Faction|de|Matsuura Fraktion|ja|松浦党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuura Faction|qq=}} and local fishermen. The current center of worship is the middle shrine. Originally, the shrine was housed in a small structure at the foot of Shijiki Mountain’s summit, but it was relocated to the mid-slope in {{ill|Yōroku|de|Yōroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōroku|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1559|de|1559|ja|1559年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1559|qq=}}). Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1961|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|qq=}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974|qq=}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} ==Merged second translation== == Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) == '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志々伎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Noko Town|ja|野子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noko Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirado, Nagasaki|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado, Nagasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also written as '''Shishiki Shrine''' or '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志志伎神社 or 志自伎神社). It is listed as a '''small-scale''' {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and under the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', it held the status of a '''former prefectural shrine'''. Several branch shrines bearing the name '''Shijiki''', '''Shishiki''', or '''Shijiki Shrine''' exist throughout Kyushu. As the '''largest of Nagasaki's Shikinaisha''', it has played a significant historical role. The '''sacred sumo ritual''' practiced here is believed to be an early form of '''sumo wrestling'''[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 Shijiki Shrine]. Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia, JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3, Nasubi Historical Archive. Archived from [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html the original] on May 18, 2003.. The '''Hirado Jangara Dance''' (平戸のジャンガラ), a local folk performance seen across '''Hirado Island''', is said to have originated from this shrine. == Overview == The founding date of '''Shijiki Shrine''' is uncertain, though some sources suggest it dates back to the '''5th century'''. The shrine’s '''sacred domain''' is centered on '''Mount Shijiki''' (志々伎山), located near the '''southern tip of Hirado Island'''. Traditionally, the shrine was divided into four main sanctuaries: * '''Upper Shrine (Kami-no-Miya)''': Located at the '''summit''' of Mount Shijiki. * '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''': Located '''midway up the mountain'''. * '''Lower Shrine (Chi-no-Miya)''': Located at '''Miyanoura''', a coastal fishing village. * '''Offshore Shrine (Oki-no-Miya)''': Located on '''Okinoshima''', an island off the coast. Additionally, the '''Enman-ji Temple''' (円満寺) historically served as the shrine’s '''bettō-ji''' (shrine-temple complex before the Meiji-era Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''deity enshrined''' here is '''Tokowake-no-Ō''' (十城別王), also known as '''Tokowake-no-Mikoto''' (十城別命), the '''son of Yamato Takeru''' and the '''half-brother of Emperor Chūai'''. He is believed to have '''accompanied Empress Jingū on her conquest of Korea''' and was later '''assigned to defend Western Japan'''. According to legend: * '''Chi-no-Miya''' (Lower Shrine) was originally a '''military weapons storehouse''' established by Tokowake-no-Ō. * '''Oki-no-Miya''' (Offshore Shrine) is said to '''house his tomb'''. Shijiki Shrine is listed in the '''Engishiki''' (延喜式) as '''one of only four Shikinaisha''' in '''Hizen Province'''. Excluding the '''Iki and Tsushima regions''', it is the '''only Engishiki shrine in modern Nagasaki Prefecture'''. Historically, it attracted strong patronage from the '''Matsura clan''' (松浦党) and the surrounding '''maritime communities'''. Today, the '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''' is the primary place of worship. The shrine originally '''stood at the mountain’s peak''', but in '''1559 (Eiroku 2)''', it was moved '''midway down the mountain'''. In '''1961 (Shōwa 36)''', it was '''relocated again''' to the '''former site of Enman-ji Temple''', which had been abolished following the '''Meiji-era Shinbutsu Bunri''' (Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''original shrine site''' was designated a '''Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site''' in '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' as the '''"Former Site of the Shikinai Shrine Shijiki Shrine"'''. == References == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === Citations === {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Shinto Shrine |Name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) |image=[[File:Shijiki-jinja, Hirado 202405-1.jpg|250px]] |Location={{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City|qq=}} Nogomachi 251 |Coordinates={{Wiki Coordinates Two-Level DMS|33|10|44.15|N|129|22|08.55|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} |Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small)
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Main Hall= |Alternate name= |Temple sites, etc.= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map={{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=place-of-worship}}

It is also referred to as Shijiki Shrine or Shiji Shrine. Ancient deity sumo, preserved here, is said to be the precursor to modern {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumo|ja|相撲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumo|qq=}}.[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 志々伎神社]. 角川日本地名大辞典.JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3. Nasubi History Museum. The sacred precinct centers on {{ill|Shijiki Mountain|de|Shijiki Berg|ja|志々伎山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijiki Mountain|qq=}} (with an elevation of 347.2 {{ill|meter|de|Meter|ja|メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter|qq=}}) near the southern tip of {{ill|Hirado Island|de|Hirado-Insel|ja|平戸島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado Island|qq=}}. In addition, a bettō-ji known as Enman-ji was established. The enshrined deity is the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|de|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, and the half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}. In the Divine Name List of the {{ill|Engi-shiki|de|Engi-shiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki|qq=}}, within {{ill|Hizen Province|de|Provinz Hizen|ja|肥前国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen Province|qq=}}, it is listed as one of the four {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}; it is the only shrine of its kind in Nagasaki Prefecture, excluding the Iki and Tsushima regions, and is the oldest known shrine. It has long garnered widespread reverence from the {{ill|Matsuura Faction|de|Matsuura Fraktion|ja|松浦党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuura Faction|qq=}} and local fishermen. The current center of worship is the middle shrine. Originally, the shrine was housed in a small structure at the foot of Shijiki Mountain’s summit, but it was relocated to the mid-slope in {{ill|Yōroku|de|Yōroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōroku|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1559|de|1559|ja|1559年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1559|qq=}}). Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1961|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|qq=}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974|qq=}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} ==Merged second translation== == Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) == '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志々伎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Noko Town|ja|野子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noko Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirado, Nagasaki|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado, Nagasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also written as '''Shishiki Shrine''' or '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志志伎神社 or 志自伎神社). It is listed as a '''small-scale''' {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and under the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', it held the status of a '''former prefectural shrine'''. Several branch shrines bearing the name '''Shijiki''', '''Shishiki''', or '''Shijiki Shrine''' exist throughout Kyushu. As the '''largest of Nagasaki's Shikinaisha''', it has played a significant historical role. The '''sacred sumo ritual''' practiced here is believed to be an early form of '''sumo wrestling'''[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 Shijiki Shrine]. Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia, JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3, Nasubi Historical Archive. Archived from [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html the original] on May 18, 2003.. The '''Hirado Jangara Dance''' (平戸のジャンガラ), a local folk performance seen across '''Hirado Island''', is said to have originated from this shrine. == Overview == The founding date of '''Shijiki Shrine''' is uncertain, though some sources suggest it dates back to the '''5th century'''. The shrine’s '''sacred domain''' is centered on '''Mount Shijiki''' (志々伎山), located near the '''southern tip of Hirado Island'''. Traditionally, the shrine was divided into four main sanctuaries: * '''Upper Shrine (Kami-no-Miya)''': Located at the '''summit''' of Mount Shijiki. * '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''': Located '''midway up the mountain'''. * '''Lower Shrine (Chi-no-Miya)''': Located at '''Miyanoura''', a coastal fishing village. * '''Offshore Shrine (Oki-no-Miya)''': Located on '''Okinoshima''', an island off the coast. Additionally, the '''Enman-ji Temple''' (円満寺) historically served as the shrine’s '''bettō-ji''' (shrine-temple complex before the Meiji-era Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''deity enshrined''' here is '''Tokowake-no-Ō''' (十城別王), also known as '''Tokowake-no-Mikoto''' (十城別命), the '''son of Yamato Takeru''' and the '''half-brother of Emperor Chūai'''. He is believed to have '''accompanied Empress Jingū on her conquest of Korea''' and was later '''assigned to defend Western Japan'''. According to legend: * '''Chi-no-Miya''' (Lower Shrine) was originally a '''military weapons storehouse''' established by Tokowake-no-Ō. * '''Oki-no-Miya''' (Offshore Shrine) is said to '''house his tomb'''. Shijiki Shrine is listed in the '''Engishiki''' (延喜式) as '''one of only four Shikinaisha''' in '''Hizen Province'''. Excluding the '''Iki and Tsushima regions''', it is the '''only Engishiki shrine in modern Nagasaki Prefecture'''. Historically, it attracted strong patronage from the '''Matsura clan''' (松浦党) and the surrounding '''maritime communities'''. Today, the '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''' is the primary place of worship. The shrine originally '''stood at the mountain’s peak''', but in '''1559 (Eiroku 2)''', it was moved '''midway down the mountain'''. In '''1961 (Shōwa 36)''', it was '''relocated again''' to the '''former site of Enman-ji Temple''', which had been abolished following the '''Meiji-era Shinbutsu Bunri''' (Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''original shrine site''' was designated a '''Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site''' in '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' as the '''"Former Site of the Shikinai Shrine Shijiki Shrine"'''. == References == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === Citations === {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Shinto Shrine |Name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) |image=[[File:Shijiki-jinja, Hirado 202405-1.jpg|250px]] |Location={{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City|qq=}} Nogomachi 251 |Coordinates={{Wiki Coordinates Two-Level DMS|33|10|44.15|N|129|22|08.55|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} |Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small)
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Main Hall= |Alternate name= |Temple sites, etc.= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map={{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=place-of-worship}}
{{Right|{{Location map|Japan Nagasaki#Japan|label=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=Location of Shijiki Shrine}}}} }} '''Shijiki Shrine''' (Shijikijinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Nogomachi, {{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City|qq=}} in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also referred to as Shijiki Shrine or Shiji Shrine. It holds the status of a {{ill|Engi-shiki Divine Registry|de|Engi-shiki Göttliche Namensliste|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Small Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Kleine Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki Divine Registry|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and a {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|de|Modernes Schreinsystem|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Old Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格|lt_de=Alte Schreinsystem|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} shrine, designating it as a prefectural shrine. Branch shrines exist throughout Kyushu under the names Shijiki, Shishiki, and Shiji, making it the largest among prefectural Shikinaisha. Ancient deity sumo, preserved here, is said to be the precursor to modern {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumo|ja|相撲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumo|qq=}}.[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 志々伎神社]. 角川日本地名大辞典.JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3. Nasubi History Museum. As of May 18, 2003, the [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Original]{{リンク切れ|date=2022年2月}} archive The Jangara, performed throughout Hirado Island, also spread from this shrine. == Overview == It is said that the shrine was founded around the 5th century, though the exact date remains unknown. The sacred precinct centers on {{ill|Shijiki Mountain|de|Shijiki Berg|ja|志々伎山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijiki Mountain|qq=}} (with an elevation of 347.2 {{ill|meter|de|Meter|ja|メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter|qq=}}) near the southern tip of {{ill|Hirado Island|de|Hirado-Insel|ja|平戸島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado Island|qq=}}. In ancient times, the upper shrine was located at the mountain summit, the middle shrine on the mid-slope, and the ground shrine (also known as the lower shrine or Hentomiyashiro) was situated in the fishing village of Miyano-ura at the foot, while an offshore shrine was placed on an islet off Miyano-ura. In addition, a bettō-ji known as Enman-ji was established. The enshrined deity is the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|de|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, and the half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}. He is said to have followed {{ill|Empress Jingū|de|Kaiserin Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} in the {{ill|Campaigns against the Three Han|de|Drei-Han-Feldzüge|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaigns against the Three Han|qq=}}, later remaining in the area and being commissioned to guard the western regions, known as {{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} ({{ill|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|de|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|ja|十城別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|qq=}})). The ground shrine is said to have repurposed an armory established by the king, while the offshore shrine is believed to mark his tomb. In the Divine Name List of the {{ill|Engi-shiki|de|Engi-shiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki|qq=}}, within {{ill|Hizen Province|de|Provinz Hizen|ja|肥前国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen Province|qq=}}, it is listed as one of the four {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}; it is the only shrine of its kind in Nagasaki Prefecture, excluding the Iki and Tsushima regions, and is the oldest known shrine. It has long garnered widespread reverence from the {{ill|Matsuura Faction|de|Matsuura Fraktion|ja|松浦党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuura Faction|qq=}} and local fishermen. The current center of worship is the middle shrine. Originally, the shrine was housed in a small structure at the foot of Shijiki Mountain’s summit, but it was relocated to the mid-slope in {{ill|Yōroku|de|Yōroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōroku|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1559|de|1559|ja|1559年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1559|qq=}}). Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1961|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|qq=}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974|qq=}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} ==Merged second translation== == Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) == '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志々伎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Noko Town|ja|野子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noko Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirado, Nagasaki|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado, Nagasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also written as '''Shishiki Shrine''' or '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志志伎神社 or 志自伎神社). It is listed as a '''small-scale''' {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and under the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', it held the status of a '''former prefectural shrine'''. Shijiki Shrine-3, Nasubi Historical Archive. Archived from [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html the original] on May 18, 2003.. Additionally, the '''Enman-ji Temple''' (円満寺) historically served as the shrine’s '''bettō-ji''' (shrine-temple complex before the Meiji-era Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''deity enshrined''' here is '''Tokowake-no-Ō''' (十城別王), also known as '''Tokowake-no-Mikoto''' (十城別命), the '''son of Yamato Takeru''' and the '''half-brother of Emperor Chūai'''. revision.text:
'''Shijiki Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Hizen Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Main Hall= |Alternate name= |Temple sites, etc.= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map={{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=place-of-worship}}
{{Right|{{Location map|Japan Nagasaki#Japan|label=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=Location of Shijiki Shrine}}}} }} '''Shijiki Shrine''' (Shijikijinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Nogomachi, {{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City|qq=}} in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also referred to as Shijiki Shrine or Shiji Shrine. It holds the status of a {{ill|Engi-shiki Divine Registry|de|Engi-shiki Göttliche Namensliste|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Small Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Kleine Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki Divine Registry|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and a {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|de|Modernes Schreinsystem|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Old Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格|lt_de=Alte Schreinsystem|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} shrine, designating it as a prefectural shrine. Branch shrines exist throughout Kyushu under the names Shijiki, Shishiki, and Shiji, making it the largest among prefectural Shikinaisha. Ancient deity sumo, preserved here, is said to be the precursor to modern {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumo|ja|相撲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumo|qq=}}.[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 志々伎神社]. 角川日本地名大辞典.JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3. Nasubi History Museum. As of May 18, 2003, the [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Original]{{リンク切れ|date=2022年2月}} archive The Jangara, performed throughout Hirado Island, also spread from this shrine. == Overview == It is said that the shrine was founded around the 5th century, though the exact date remains unknown. The sacred precinct centers on {{ill|Shijiki Mountain|de|Shijiki Berg|ja|志々伎山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijiki Mountain|qq=}} (with an elevation of 347.2 {{ill|meter|de|Meter|ja|メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter|qq=}}) near the southern tip of {{ill|Hirado Island|de|Hirado-Insel|ja|平戸島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado Island|qq=}}. In ancient times, the upper shrine was located at the mountain summit, the middle shrine on the mid-slope, and the ground shrine (also known as the lower shrine or Hentomiyashiro) was situated in the fishing village of Miyano-ura at the foot, while an offshore shrine was placed on an islet off Miyano-ura. In addition, a bettō-ji known as Enman-ji was established. The enshrined deity is the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|de|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, and the half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}. He is said to have followed {{ill|Empress Jingū|de|Kaiserin Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} in the {{ill|Campaigns against the Three Han|de|Drei-Han-Feldzüge|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaigns against the Three Han|qq=}}, later remaining in the area and being commissioned to guard the western regions, known as {{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} ({{ill|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|de|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|ja|十城別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|qq=}})). The ground shrine is said to have repurposed an armory established by the king, while the offshore shrine is believed to mark his tomb. In the Divine Name List of the {{ill|Engi-shiki|de|Engi-shiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki|qq=}}, within {{ill|Hizen Province|de|Provinz Hizen|ja|肥前国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen Province|qq=}}, it is listed as one of the four {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}; it is the only shrine of its kind in Nagasaki Prefecture, excluding the Iki and Tsushima regions, and is the oldest known shrine. It has long garnered widespread reverence from the {{ill|Matsuura Faction|de|Matsuura Fraktion|ja|松浦党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuura Faction|qq=}} and local fishermen. The current center of worship is the middle shrine. Originally, the shrine was housed in a small structure at the foot of Shijiki Mountain’s summit, but it was relocated to the mid-slope in {{ill|Yōroku|de|Yōroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōroku|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1559|de|1559|ja|1559年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1559|qq=}}). Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1961|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|qq=}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974|qq=}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} ==Merged second translation== == Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) == '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志々伎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Noko Town|ja|野子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noko Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirado, Nagasaki|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado, Nagasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also written as '''Shishiki Shrine''' or '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志志伎神社 or 志自伎神社). It is listed as a '''small-scale''' {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and under the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', it held the status of a '''former prefectural shrine'''. Several branch shrines bearing the name '''Shijiki''', '''Shishiki''', or '''Shijiki Shrine''' exist throughout Kyushu. As the '''largest of Nagasaki's Shikinaisha''', it has played a significant historical role. The '''sacred sumo ritual''' practiced here is believed to be an early form of '''sumo wrestling'''[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 Shijiki Shrine]. Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia, JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3, Nasubi Historical Archive. Archived from [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html the original] on May 18, 2003.. The '''Hirado Jangara Dance''' (平戸のジャンガラ), a local folk performance seen across '''Hirado Island''', is said to have originated from this shrine. == Overview == The founding date of '''Shijiki Shrine''' is uncertain, though some sources suggest it dates back to the '''5th century'''. The shrine’s '''sacred domain''' is centered on '''Mount Shijiki''' (志々伎山), located near the '''southern tip of Hirado Island'''. Traditionally, the shrine was divided into four main sanctuaries: * '''Upper Shrine (Kami-no-Miya)''': Located at the '''summit''' of Mount Shijiki. * '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''': Located '''midway up the mountain'''. * '''Lower Shrine (Chi-no-Miya)''': Located at '''Miyanoura''', a coastal fishing village. * '''Offshore Shrine (Oki-no-Miya)''': Located on '''Okinoshima''', an island off the coast. Additionally, the '''Enman-ji Temple''' (円満寺) historically served as the shrine’s '''bettō-ji''' (shrine-temple complex before the Meiji-era Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''deity enshrined''' here is '''Tokowake-no-Ō''' (十城別王), also known as '''Tokowake-no-Mikoto''' (十城別命), the '''son of Yamato Takeru''' and the '''half-brother of Emperor Chūai'''. He is believed to have '''accompanied Empress Jingū on her conquest of Korea''' and was later '''assigned to defend Western Japan'''. According to legend: * '''Chi-no-Miya''' (Lower Shrine) was originally a '''military weapons storehouse''' established by Tokowake-no-Ō. * '''Oki-no-Miya''' (Offshore Shrine) is said to '''house his tomb'''. Shijiki Shrine is listed in the '''Engishiki''' (延喜式) as '''one of only four Shikinaisha''' in '''Hizen Province'''. Excluding the '''Iki and Tsushima regions''', it is the '''only Engishiki shrine in modern Nagasaki Prefecture'''. Historically, it attracted strong patronage from the '''Matsura clan''' (松浦党) and the surrounding '''maritime communities'''. Today, the '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''' is the primary place of worship. The shrine originally '''stood at the mountain’s peak''', but in '''1559 (Eiroku 2)''', it was moved '''midway down the mountain'''. In '''1961 (Shōwa 36)''', it was '''relocated again''' to the '''former site of Enman-ji Temple''', which had been abolished following the '''Meiji-era Shinbutsu Bunri''' (Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''original shrine site''' was designated a '''Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site''' in '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' as the '''"Former Site of the Shikinai Shrine Shijiki Shrine"'''. == References == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === Citations === {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Shinto Shrine |Name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) |image=[[File:Shijiki-jinja, Hirado 202405-1.jpg|250px]] |Location={{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City|qq=}} Nogomachi 251 |Coordinates={{Wiki Coordinates Two-Level DMS|33|10|44.15|N|129|22|08.55|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} |Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small)

Originally, the shrine was housed in a small structure at the foot of Shijiki Mountain’s summit, but it was relocated to the mid-slope in {{ill|Yōroku|de|Yōroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōroku|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1559|de|1559|ja|1559年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1559|qq=}}). Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1961|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|qq=}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974|qq=}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} ==Merged second translation== == Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) == '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志々伎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Noko Town|ja|野子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noko Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirado, Nagasaki|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado, Nagasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also written as '''Shishiki Shrine''' or '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志志伎神社 or 志自伎神社). It is listed as a '''small-scale''' {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and under the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', it held the status of a '''former prefectural shrine'''. Several branch shrines bearing the name '''Shijiki''', '''Shishiki''', or '''Shijiki Shrine''' exist throughout Kyushu. As the '''largest of Nagasaki's Shikinaisha''', it has played a significant historical role. The '''sacred sumo ritual''' practiced here is believed to be an early form of '''sumo wrestling'''[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 Shijiki Shrine]. Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia, JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3, Nasubi Historical Archive. Archived from [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html the original] on May 18, 2003.. The '''Hirado Jangara Dance''' (平戸のジャンガラ), a local folk performance seen across '''Hirado Island''', is said to have originated from this shrine. == Overview == The founding date of '''Shijiki Shrine''' is uncertain, though some sources suggest it dates back to the '''5th century'''. The shrine’s '''sacred domain''' is centered on '''Mount Shijiki''' (志々伎山), located near the '''southern tip of Hirado Island'''. Traditionally, the shrine was divided into four main sanctuaries: * '''Upper Shrine (Kami-no-Miya)''': Located at the '''summit''' of Mount Shijiki. * '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''': Located '''midway up the mountain'''. * '''Lower Shrine (Chi-no-Miya)''': Located at '''Miyanoura''', a coastal fishing village. * '''Offshore Shrine (Oki-no-Miya)''': Located on '''Okinoshima''', an island off the coast. Additionally, the '''Enman-ji Temple''' (円満寺) historically served as the shrine’s '''bettō-ji''' (shrine-temple complex before the Meiji-era Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''deity enshrined''' here is '''Tokowake-no-Ō''' (十城別王), also known as '''Tokowake-no-Mikoto''' (十城別命), the '''son of Yamato Takeru''' and the '''half-brother of Emperor Chūai'''. He is believed to have '''accompanied Empress Jingū on her conquest of Korea''' and was later '''assigned to defend Western Japan'''. According to legend: * '''Chi-no-Miya''' (Lower Shrine) was originally a '''military weapons storehouse''' established by Tokowake-no-Ō. * '''Oki-no-Miya''' (Offshore Shrine) is said to '''house his tomb'''. Shijiki Shrine is listed in the '''Engishiki''' (延喜式) as '''one of only four Shikinaisha''' in '''Hizen Province'''. Excluding the '''Iki and Tsushima regions''', it is the '''only Engishiki shrine in modern Nagasaki Prefecture'''. Historically, it attracted strong patronage from the '''Matsura clan''' (松浦党) and the surrounding '''maritime communities'''. Today, the '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''' is the primary place of worship. The shrine originally '''stood at the mountain’s peak''', but in '''1559 (Eiroku 2)''', it was moved '''midway down the mountain'''. In '''1961 (Shōwa 36)''', it was '''relocated again''' to the '''former site of Enman-ji Temple''', which had been abolished following the '''Meiji-era Shinbutsu Bunri''' (Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''original shrine site''' was designated a '''Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site''' in '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' as the '''"Former Site of the Shikinai Shrine Shijiki Shrine"'''. == References == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === Citations === {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Shinto Shrine |Name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) |image=[[File:Shijiki-jinja, Hirado 202405-1.jpg|250px]] |Location={{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City|qq=}} Nogomachi 251 |Coordinates={{Wiki Coordinates Two-Level DMS|33|10|44.15|N|129|22|08.55|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} |Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small)
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Main Hall= |Alternate name= |Temple sites, etc.= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map={{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=place-of-worship}}
{{Right|{{Location map|Japan Nagasaki#Japan|label=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=Location of Shijiki Shrine}}}} }} '''Shijiki Shrine''' (Shijikijinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Nogomachi, {{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City|qq=}} in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also referred to as Shijiki Shrine or Shiji Shrine. It holds the status of a {{ill|Engi-shiki Divine Registry|de|Engi-shiki Göttliche Namensliste|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Small Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Kleine Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki Divine Registry|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and a {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|de|Modernes Schreinsystem|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Old Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格|lt_de=Alte Schreinsystem|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} shrine, designating it as a prefectural shrine. Branch shrines exist throughout Kyushu under the names Shijiki, Shishiki, and Shiji, making it the largest among prefectural Shikinaisha. Ancient deity sumo, preserved here, is said to be the precursor to modern {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumo|ja|相撲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumo|qq=}}.[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 志々伎神社]. 角川日本地名大辞典.JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3. Nasubi History Museum. As of May 18, 2003, the [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Original]{{リンク切れ|date=2022年2月}} archive The Jangara, performed throughout Hirado Island, also spread from this shrine. == Overview == It is said that the shrine was founded around the 5th century, though the exact date remains unknown. The sacred precinct centers on {{ill|Shijiki Mountain|de|Shijiki Berg|ja|志々伎山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijiki Mountain|qq=}} (with an elevation of 347.2 {{ill|meter|de|Meter|ja|メートル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter|qq=}}) near the southern tip of {{ill|Hirado Island|de|Hirado-Insel|ja|平戸島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado Island|qq=}}. In ancient times, the upper shrine was located at the mountain summit, the middle shrine on the mid-slope, and the ground shrine (also known as the lower shrine or Hentomiyashiro) was situated in the fishing village of Miyano-ura at the foot, while an offshore shrine was placed on an islet off Miyano-ura. In addition, a bettō-ji known as Enman-ji was established. The enshrined deity is the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|de|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, and the half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}. He is said to have followed {{ill|Empress Jingū|de|Kaiserin Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} in the {{ill|Campaigns against the Three Han|de|Drei-Han-Feldzüge|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaigns against the Three Han|qq=}}, later remaining in the area and being commissioned to guard the western regions, known as {{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} ({{ill|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|de|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|ja|十城別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|qq=}})). The ground shrine is said to have repurposed an armory established by the king, while the offshore shrine is believed to mark his tomb. In the Divine Name List of the {{ill|Engi-shiki|de|Engi-shiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki|qq=}}, within {{ill|Hizen Province|de|Provinz Hizen|ja|肥前国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen Province|qq=}}, it is listed as one of the four {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}; it is the only shrine of its kind in Nagasaki Prefecture, excluding the Iki and Tsushima regions, and is the oldest known shrine. It has long garnered widespread reverence from the {{ill|Matsuura Faction|de|Matsuura Fraktion|ja|松浦党|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuura Faction|qq=}} and local fishermen. The current center of worship is the middle shrine. Originally, the shrine was housed in a small structure at the foot of Shijiki Mountain’s summit, but it was relocated to the mid-slope in {{ill|Yōroku|de|Yōroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōroku|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1559|de|1559|ja|1559年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1559|qq=}}). Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1961|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|qq=}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974|qq=}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} ==Merged second translation== == Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) == '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志々伎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Noko Town|ja|野子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noko Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirado, Nagasaki|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado, Nagasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is located in [[Hizen Province]]. {{Shinto Shrine |Name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) |image=[[File:Shijiki-jinja, Hirado 202405-1.jpg|250px]] |Location={{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City|qq=}} Nogomachi 251 |Coordinates={{Wiki Coordinates Two-Level DMS|33|10|44.15|N|129|22|08.55|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} |Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small)

{{Right|{{Location map|Japan Nagasaki#Japan|label=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=Location of Shijiki Shrine}}}} }} '''Shijiki Shrine''' (Shijikijinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Nogomachi, {{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City}} in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is also referred to as Shijiki Shrine or Shiji Shrine. It holds the status of a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|zh|延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Small Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Kleine Shikinaisha|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and a {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|de|Modernes Schreinsystem|ja|近代社格制度|lt=Old Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格|lt_de=Alte Schreinsystem|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} shrine, designating it as a prefectural shrine. Branch shrines exist throughout Kyushu under the names Shijiki, Shishiki, and Shiji, making it the largest among prefectural Shikinaisha. Ancient deity sumo, preserved here, is said to be the precursor to modern {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumo|ja|相撲|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumo}}.[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 志々伎神社]. 角川日本地名大辞典.JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3. Nasubi History Museum. As of May 18, 2003, the [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Original]{{リンク切れ|date=2022年2月}} archive The Jangara, performed throughout Hirado Island, also spread from this shrine. == Overview == It is said that the shrine was founded around the 5th century, though the exact date remains unknown. The sacred precinct centers on {{ill|Shijiki Mountain|de|Shijiki Berg|ja|志々伎山|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijiki Mountain}} (with an elevation of 347.2 {{ill|meter|de|Meter|ja|メートル|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter}}) near the southern tip of {{ill|Hirado Island|de|Hirado-Insel|ja|平戸島|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado Island}}. In ancient times, the upper shrine was located at the mountain summit, the middle shrine on the mid-slope, and the ground shrine (also known as the lower shrine or Hentomiyashiro) was situated in the fishing village of Miyano-ura at the foot, while an offshore shrine was placed on an islet off Miyano-ura. In addition, a bettō-ji known as Enman-ji was established. The enshrined deity is the son of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|de|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}}, and the half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai}}. He is said to have followed {{ill|Empress Jingū|de|Kaiserin Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū}} in the {{ill|Campaigns against the Three Han|de|Drei-Han-Feldzüge|ja|三韓征伐|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaigns against the Three Han}}, later remaining in the area and being commissioned to guard the western regions, known as {{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō}} ({{ill|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|de|Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto|ja|十城別命|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-no-mikoto}})). The ground shrine is said to have repurposed an armory established by the king, while the offshore shrine is believed to mark his tomb. In the Divine Name List of the {{ill|Engi-shiki|de|Engi-shiki|ja|延喜式|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi-shiki}}, within {{ill|Hizen Province|de|Provinz Hizen|ja|肥前国|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen Province}}, it is listed as one of the four {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|ja|式内社|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha}}; it is the only shrine of its kind in Nagasaki Prefecture, excluding the Iki and Tsushima regions, and is the oldest known shrine. It has long garnered widespread reverence from the {{ill|Matsuura Faction|de|Matsuura Fraktion|ja|松浦党|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuura Faction}} and local fishermen. The current center of worship is the middle shrine. Originally, the shrine was housed in a small structure at the foot of Shijiki Mountain’s summit, but it was relocated to the mid-slope in {{ill|Yōroku|de|Yōroku|ja|永禄|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōroku}} 2 ({{ill|1559|de|1559|ja|1559年|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1559}}). Furthermore, in {{ill|1961|de|1961|ja|1961年|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1961}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa}} 36), it was moved to its present location, which occupies the former site of Enman-ji—a bettō-ji abolished in the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict|de|Trennung von Shinto und Buddhismus Dekret|ja|神仏分離令|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto-Buddhism Separation Edict}}. The former shrine grounds were designated as a historical site of Nagasaki Prefecture in {{ill|1974|de|1974|ja|1974年|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974}} (Showa 49), under the name "Former Shikinaisha Shijiki Shrine Site." == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Matsura-gun|en|Matsura-gun|ja|松浦郡|ko|마쓰라군|zh|松浦郡|lt=Matsura district|WD=Q5973791}} || {{ill|Shijiki Shrine|lt=Shijiki Shrine|ja|志々伎神社|commons|Category:Shijiki-jinja|WD=Q11490808}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|33.178307|129.369101|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183317 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11490808}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Shijiki-jinja}} * {{ill|List of Shrines|de|Liste der Schreine|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Authority control}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} ==Merged second translation== == Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) == '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志々伎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Noko Town|ja|野子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noko Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirado, Nagasaki|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado, Nagasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also written as '''Shishiki Shrine''' or '''Shijiki Shrine''' (志志伎神社 or 志自伎神社). It is listed as a '''small-scale''' {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and under the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', it held the status of a '''former prefectural shrine'''. Several branch shrines bearing the name '''Shijiki''', '''Shishiki''', or '''Shijiki Shrine''' exist throughout Kyushu. As the '''largest of Nagasaki's Shikinaisha''', it has played a significant historical role. The '''sacred sumo ritual''' practiced here is believed to be an early form of '''sumo wrestling'''[http://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7221037 Shijiki Shrine]. Kadokawa Japan Place Name Encyclopedia, JLogos.com“[https://web.archive.org/web/20030518172110/http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html Shijiki Shrine Festival]”. Shijiki Shrine-3, Nasubi Historical Archive. Archived from [http://www53.tok2.com/home/nasubi/history/shjikijinja/shijikijinja3.html the original] on May 18, 2003.. The '''Hirado Jangara Dance''' (平戸のジャンガラ), a local folk performance seen across '''Hirado Island''', is said to have originated from this shrine. == Overview == The founding date of '''Shijiki Shrine''' is uncertain, though some sources suggest it dates back to the '''5th century'''. The shrine’s '''sacred domain''' is centered on '''Mount Shijiki''' (志々伎山), located near the '''southern tip of Hirado Island'''. Traditionally, the shrine was divided into four main sanctuaries: * '''Upper Shrine (Kami-no-Miya)''': Located at the '''summit''' of Mount Shijiki. * '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''': Located '''midway up the mountain'''. * '''Lower Shrine (Chi-no-Miya)''': Located at '''Miyanoura''', a coastal fishing village. * '''Offshore Shrine (Oki-no-Miya)''': Located on '''Okinoshima''', an island off the coast. Additionally, the '''Enman-ji Temple''' (円満寺) historically served as the shrine’s '''bettō-ji''' (shrine-temple complex before the Meiji-era Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''deity enshrined''' here is '''Tokowake-no-Ō''' (十城別王), also known as '''Tokowake-no-Mikoto''' (十城別命), the '''son of Yamato Takeru''' and the '''half-brother of Emperor Chūai'''. He is believed to have '''accompanied Empress Jingū on her conquest of Korea''' and was later '''assigned to defend Western Japan'''. According to legend: * '''Chi-no-Miya''' (Lower Shrine) was originally a '''military weapons storehouse''' established by Tokowake-no-Ō. * '''Oki-no-Miya''' (Offshore Shrine) is said to '''house his tomb'''. Shijiki Shrine is listed in the '''Engishiki''' (延喜式) as '''one of only four Shikinaisha''' in '''Hizen Province'''. Excluding the '''Iki and Tsushima regions''', it is the '''only Engishiki shrine in modern Nagasaki Prefecture'''. Historically, it attracted strong patronage from the '''Matsura clan''' (松浦党) and the surrounding '''maritime communities'''. Today, the '''Middle Shrine (Naka-no-Miya)''' is the primary place of worship. The shrine originally '''stood at the mountain’s peak''', but in '''1559 (Eiroku 2)''', it was moved '''midway down the mountain'''. In '''1961 (Shōwa 36)''', it was '''relocated again''' to the '''former site of Enman-ji Temple''', which had been abolished following the '''Meiji-era Shinbutsu Bunri''' (Shinto-Buddhist separation). The '''original shrine site''' was designated a '''Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site''' in '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' as the '''"Former Site of the Shikinai Shrine Shijiki Shrine"'''. == References == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === Citations === {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Japan|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|志々伎神社|version=104096595|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Shinto Shrine |Name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社) |image=[[File:Shijiki-jinja, Hirado 202405-1.jpg|250px]] |Location={{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nagasaki|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Hirado City|de|Stadt Hirado|ja|平戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirado City|qq=}} Nogomachi 251 |Coordinates={{Wiki Coordinates Two-Level DMS|33|10|44.15|N|129|22|08.55|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shijiki Shrine (志々伎神社)}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|de|Jūjōbetsu-Ō|ja|十城別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjōbetsu-Ō|qq=}} |Shrine Rank=Shikinaisha (small)
